rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and insert annex content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Keep component-type contract content separate from implementation-scope content; do not move implementation-only material into a type owner.
Respect scope visibility: only symbols valid in the selected owner may appear there, and implementation-local paths must remain in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Use owner-consistent insertion so added content does not alter subcomponents, connections, features, or properties.
When merging, retain existing declarations and structural elements unchanged unless the new annex content explicitly extends the same owner.
Prefer minimal edits that add the new annex block or merge with an existing annex for the same owner without restructuring unrelated model text.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is carried in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block attached to a component type or implementation owner.
Implementation-level content belongs after relevant structural declarations in that implementation scope when such scope dependencies exist.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-dependent references in a component type owner.
Do not modify architecture, ownership, or structural declarations just to accommodate annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal equation syntax and logical operator details that matter to clause generation rather than owner/scope fusion.
Requirement-specific threshold or range-pattern guidance not needed for owner-preserving insertion.
Scalar helper type diagnostics unless fusion changes declaration scope or ownership.
